questions.text: Which of the following are the raw materials required for photosynthesis in autotrophs?
options: Carbon dioxide, Water, Chlorophyll and Sunlight
questions.explanation: Autotrophs use inorganic materials like CO2 and H2O in the presence of sunlight and chlorophyll to synthesize organic food.

questions.text: The site of photosynthesis in the cells of a leaf is:
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll, the green pigment that traps solar energy for photosynthesis.

questions.text: The opening and closing of the stomatal pore is a function of the:
questions.explanation: Guard cells swell when water flows into them, causing the stomatal pore to open, and shrink when they lose water.

questions.text: In which organism does the breakdown of pyruvate using oxygen take place in the mitochondria?
questions.explanation: In aerobic respiration, the 3-carbon pyruvate molecule is broken down into CO2 and water within the mitochondria.

questions.text: Amoeba takes in food using temporary finger-like extensions of the cell surface called:
questions.explanation: Pseudopodia fuse over the food particle to form a food vacuole for digestion.

questions.text: Which enzyme present in saliva breaks down starch into sugar?
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase begins the chemical digestion of carbohydrates in the mouth.

questions.text: The internal energy reserve in autotrophs is:
questions.explanation: Plants store excess carbohydrates in the form of starch, while animals store it as glycogen.

questions.text: What is the role of Hydrochloric acid (HCl) in the human stomach?
options: To create an acidic medium for pepsin
questions.explanation: HCl creates an acidic environment necessary for the protein-digesting enzyme pepsin to function.

questions.text: The secretion of the liver that helps in the emulsification of fats is:
questions.explanation: Bile salts break down large fat globules into smaller ones, increasing enzyme efficiency.

questions.text: The longest part of the alimentary canal in humans is the:
questions.explanation: The small intestine is long and highly coiled to provide a large surface area for absorption.

questions.text: The process of digestion in the small intestine is completed by enzymes secreted by:
questions.explanation: The small intestine receives secretions from the liver and pancreas, and its own walls secrete intestinal juice.

questions.text: The inner lining of the small intestine has numerous finger-like projections called:
questions.explanation: Villi increase the surface area for the absorption of digested food into the blood.

questions.text: In yeast, the breakdown of pyruvate during anaerobic respiration results in:
questions.explanation: Fermentation in yeast converts pyruvate into ethanol and carbon dioxide in the absence of oxygen.

questions.text: Which of the following products are formed during anaerobic respiration in our muscle cells?
questions.explanation: During vigorous exercise, lack of oxygen leads to lactic acid buildup in muscles, causing cramps.

questions.text: The first step of respiration is the breakdown of glucose (6-carbon) into pyruvate (3-carbon), which occurs in the:
questions.explanation: Glycolysis, the initial stage of respiration, takes place in the cytoplasm of all cells.

questions.text: Rings of cartilage are present in the throat to ensure that:
options: The air passage does not collapse
questions.explanation: Cartilaginous rings keep the trachea open even when there is little air in it.

questions.text: The balloon-like structures in the lungs where gas exchange takes place are called:
questions.explanation: Alveoli provide an extensive surface for the exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide with blood capillaries.

questions.text: The respiratory pigment in human beings which has a high affinity for oxygen is:
questions.explanation: Hemoglobin in red blood cells carries oxygen from the lungs to all tissues of the body.

questions.text: In the human heart, the separation of the right side and the left side allows:
options: High energy supply by separating O2 and de-O2 blood
Blood to flow to lungs only
questions.explanation: The four-chambered heart prevents the mixing of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood, essential for high metabolic needs.

questions.text: Which organisms have a three-chambered heart and can tolerate some mixing of blood?
questions.explanation: Cold-blooded animals like frogs do not need as much energy for body temperature regulation, so they have 3 chambers.

questions.text: Blood pressure is measured using an instrument called a:
questions.explanation: A sphygmomanometer measures the systolic and diastolic pressure of blood in the arteries.

questions.text: Which blood vessels have thick elastic walls to carry blood away from the heart under high pressure?
questions.explanation: Arteries carry oxygenated blood (except pulmonary) at high pressure, requiring thick walls.

questions.text: Veins contain ______ to ensure that blood flows only in one direction towards the heart.
questions.explanation: Valves prevent the backflow of blood as it moves at lower pressure in the veins.

questions.text: The blood cells responsible for plugging leaks by helping in the clotting of blood are:
questions.explanation: Platelets circulate in the blood and form clots at the site of injury to prevent blood loss.

questions.text: Lymph, also known as tissue fluid, is similar to the plasma of blood but is:
questions.explanation: Lymph escapes from capillaries into intercellular spaces; it is colorless and helps in transport and immunity.

questions.text: In plants, Xylem is responsible for the transport of:
questions.explanation: Xylem tissue consists of tracheids and vessels that form a continuous system to move water from roots upward.

questions.text: The loss of water in the form of vapor from the aerial parts of a plant is called:
questions.explanation: Transpiration creates a suction pull that helps in the upward movement of water and dissolved minerals.

questions.text: The transport of soluble products of photosynthesis (food) in plants is called:
questions.explanation: Translocation occurs in the phloem and requires energy from ATP.

questions.text: The basic filtration unit in the human kidney is the:
questions.explanation: Each kidney contains a large number of nephrons that filter waste from the blood.

questions.text: The cup-shaped end of a nephron that encloses a cluster of capillaries is the:
questions.explanation: The Bowman's capsule collects the filtrate from the glomerulus.

questions.text: During urine formation, which substances are selectively reabsorbed by the tubular part of the nephron?
options: Glucose, Amino acids, Salts and Water
questions.explanation: Useful substances are taken back into the blood, while waste remains to form urine.

questions.text: Urine is stored in the ______ until the pressure of the expanded organ leads to the urge to pass it out.
questions.explanation: The bladder is a muscular organ that holds urine until it is excreted through the urethra.

questions.text: Plants get rid of excess water through:
questions.explanation: Transpiration through stomata is the primary way plants release excess water.

questions.text: The process of using an artificial kidney to remove nitrogenous waste products from the blood is:
questions.explanation: Hemodialysis mimics kidney function for patients with kidney failure.

questions.text: Which of the following is a nitrogenous waste excreted by humans?
questions.explanation: Urea and uric acid are the main nitrogenous wastes produced by protein metabolism in humans.

questions.text: The movements of the alimentary canal to push food forward are called:
questions.explanation: Peristalsis is the rhythmic contraction and relaxation of muscles in the digestive tract.

questions.text: The enzyme trypsin, secreted by the pancreas, acts on:
questions.explanation: Trypsin helps in the digestion of proteins in the small intestine.

questions.text: What protects the inner lining of the stomach from the action of Hydrochloric acid?
questions.explanation: Mucus forms a protective layer to prevent HCl from damaging the stomach wall.

questions.text: Herbivores have a longer small intestine compared to carnivores to allow the digestion of:
questions.explanation: Cellulose in grass is difficult to digest and requires a longer path for complete breakdown.

questions.text: Energy currency for most cellular processes is:
questions.explanation: Adenosine triphosphate (ATP) provides the energy needed for endothermic reactions in cells.
